changes_made: Changed 'k8s_rbac_service_account_scope_limited' to 'k8s_rbac_service_account_scope_restricted' for consistency with existing naming conventions
Changed 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_scope_limited' to 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_scope_restricted' for consistency with existing naming conventions
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes RBAC controls, focusing on enforcing least privilege and limiting the scope of roles and bindings. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but minor adjustments were made to ensure consistency with existing function names, particularly using 'restricted' instead of 'limited' to align with common terminology.
naming_improvements: Use 'restricted' instead of 'limited' to maintain consistency with existing function names
title: (continued) Definitions Key elements of an access control model include: • Resources to be protec...
step1_reasoning: The control focuses on access control models, which directly map to Kubernetes RBAC (Role-Based Access Control). In Kubernetes, this involves ensuring that roles and bindings are configured to enforce least privilege, avoiding overly broad permissions such as cluster-admin, and ensuring that service accounts and role bindings are scoped appropriately to limit access to resources.
